item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it is an art piece by Matilde Pernille A. H. called Fake VI, and requires official purchase for use as seen here. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Approximately" 54 items? I assume the Foundation knows how to count to 54, right? There shouldn't be anything "approximate" about a simple item count.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This ancient line of puns shall not be deFEETed by time!

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Edit made, I was looking for the right word.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Maybe "intrigued" instead of "impressed", to go with that whole Fifty Shades of Squick undertone MC&D stuff often has?

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Due to the effects and GOI, I feel like a connection to SCP-1808 is warranted.

I propose we do it in this fashion.

Setting Nine "Punish." The operator of the device will be given complete control of the composition of the person within SCP-1725 via a command terminal. Any changes made to the subject's body will be extremely painful to the subject, and can lead to their death. Changes can be reverted at the operator's choice.

to:

Setting Nine "Punish." The operator of the device will be given complete control of the composition of the person within SCP-1725 via a command terminal. Any changes made to the subject's body will be extremely painful to the subject, and can lead to their death. Changes can be reverted at the operator's choice.1
Footnotes
1. It is believed that SCP-1808, recovered later in Somerset, England, was developed to cater to clientele intrigued by the demonstration of this option.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is truly disgusting, and to be honest given the real and horrifying practices of footbinding, FGM, etc. does not seem particularly 'beyond the pale', especially historically. People given power over other people often stop viewing them as fully human.

As a small note:

In addition, there is a 16% chance of the new growths becoming fused to the eyes or eyelids.

I'm not a big fan of arbitrary specificity; the implication that the Foundation ran a largely deadly scp enough times to determine a precise percentage for side-effects seems excessive. I feel like saying 'in a minority of cases the new growths become fused to the eyes or eyelids' still brings home the body horror without overspecification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Persons will grow large, sharp bone protrusions from their ears, and will gain massive restructuring of the face and body. Persons affected in this way will often have difficulty with locomotion and speech.

The problem I have with this is will gain massive restructuring of the face and body, this is not terribly specific, and the word gain isn't really appropriate here.

It would be better to explain what the changes are (I think), or at the very least change "gain" to "undergo".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Object classes are based on difficulty of containment, not the effects of the object. If you can lock it up and it won't do anything, then it's Safe.

Also, try to avoid posting multiple comments in succession. It gets spammy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this SCP safe? Reading all the settings, it makes me think it would be in Elucide or Keter….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very creative… I love the concept!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love moral hazard of the moneybags.. And the image is disgusting enough to be upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, what the fuck

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WELL this is fucked up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm picky about body-horror, but the association with MC&D actually sells this one, for me— it's sadistic without being stupidly over the top. Main criticism would be setting 9, it seems pointless and a bit heavy-handed. Everything on the list already fits that category.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea, great photo (eurgh!), poor tone. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this hits just the right balance of function and squick, actually. It's easy to imagine greedy, affluently wealthy socialites who would subject their staff to physical deformity for the sake of being fashionable. Although, the language might be changed to better give the impression that, while truly grotesque and painful, these settings do typically produce superficially beautiful results.

For example, I like what someone said about the "Heels" transformation producing hoof, rather than bone spurs. Describing the changes as producing feet with hooves shaped to appear to be high-heeled shoes (Possibly changes to the pigmentation of skin in the legs to give the appearance of stockings?), or that the hoop earring setting adds thick cartilage (rather than muscle) and gives the skin a silver, golden, or otherwise attractively coloured hue (Introduction of chitin, perhaps?), or other such detail explaining how the final results belie the actual changes being made. Perhaps rather than being option-nine specific, the console could have functions used to fine-tune each setting, for just the right kind of non-functioning eyes attached to the ear (Don't they look just like sapphire drops, Janet?) or choosing between "Elf", "Martian", and "[DATA EXPUNGED]" on the fantasy setting. Really though, to me the cruelty involved in using this thing as intended perfectly illustrates the type of clients MC&D caters to, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They aren't non-functional. Just in agony. And you can always get more slaves servants elsewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
£950000

Damn that's a lot of money to make my servants non-functional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I added a few 0's to the price.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but if we don't talk aboot it these great ouns will vanish without a lace

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Par. 3

along with approximately 54 other items.

I don't know about you, but this bugs me.
Otherwise, this is a really grim, eerie prospect to think of anyone being submitted to 1725.
+1 for having me wince many times during my reading of the addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Get your feet out of your mouthes guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do love the effects of this, and how it isn't just "poof, you change now": it's painful, there are side-effects, it can sometimes lead to death. Also, great squicky picture, even if I've seen it before.

That said, there are a lot of problems with the execution. Foremost, I think "elven" breaks tone. The "Fantasy" title coupled with a visual description of the effects will get the point across. Also, there are a lot of missing apostrophes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *arches a brow*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you mean "all you punsters, SHOE! Now!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All you punsters, SHOO! Now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Put a sock in it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoa now, don't want to start off on the wrong foot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heel, boy. No need to get balled up, just because you find this corny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the rare cases I agree with Anaxagoras. This would be a "fun" thing to experiment with, but not something that you'd want to use on actual working personnel.
Besides, the things it makes.. the foot distorsion would perhaps be a good one, if it was made out of keratin, like feet or hooves, same with a lot of the others. The elf-face is another good one. Others.. when you think about how would they work, it's sick stuff, but not in an enticing way - more like Nurgle than Slaanesh.
Overall, not a fan and downvoting , though it's perhaps good to see a MC&D item pop up once in a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I open a page and see a picture I've seen on Cracked it really breaks my suspension. Plus the article mentions scar tissue but it just looks like regular skin on the heel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Room is something that is a part of a building. Boxes can be pretty big.

What bothers me more is that it's "held in a Safe Containment locker". Unlike boxes, lockers are inherently small. This should be kept in a room instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't even bother looking at the cannibalistic food generator, look at the internet. Rule 34 has came up with weirder stuff.

That being said, I quite like this idea apart from the price, which is indeed too low for MC&D.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there some reason a box can't be that big?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am putting my (weirdly-heeled) foot down on this right now. THIS IS STUPID

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If they could mass produce them, they probably wouldn't be auctioning them off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While we're at it, shouldn't it be "£9500"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For all we know, MC&D has a supplier that can mass-produce these. If there's only one, then yes, you'd want to sell it for a minimum of a million pounds. But if you have 10,000 of them to get rid of, well… you gotta take what you can get.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It sounds far too much like something that someone would want.

… I do not want to know what goes on in your head. :P

That being said, my personal "too useful/want it" threshold is whether or not it's lacking substantial downsides. And yes, you as a customer may want this box, but you as the subject would be running away screaming from it. Every single one of these modifications is, at best, grotesquely disfiguring and, at worst, horrifically painful both during the actual transformation and in the aftermath. Just take the "high heels" setting: I don't know if you've ever had bone spurs in your ankle or heel before, but they're incredibly painful to walk on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well knowing the picture is 100% fake kind of ruins that aspect

… That is what's breaking suspension of disbelief for you? Yes, it's obviously a photoshop, but it's a good photoshop and appears realistic given the unreal subject matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldnt, you know, make the servants more terifying and horrible than "beautiful"? Then again, this comes from an organisation that has a cannibalistic food generator as one of their SCPs…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We should toe the line or admit defeet

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
9500£

This seems like way too low a price:

1) The type of customers that MC&D has would be able to pay a lot more than that for anomalous items.

2) I don't think they could stay in business if they sold anomalous items for that little.

2b) Even if they could stay in business with prices like that, MC&D would want a large profit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1725 is an ornate brass box, with an area of approximately 4x3x3 meters.

Wouldn't that be a room, not a box?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Oh, sure, some of your slaves - you have slaves, right? We all have slaves - will become misshapen and useless, so just throw them out and try it again on more slaves, because if there's one thing we all have other than slaves, it's more slaves." - Potential Customer

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let's not get hotfooted about the puns, there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm inclined to agree. Sure, MC&D's clients may be sadistic, but they also have some pragmatism. All of these would make it nearly impossible for the employees to do their jobs, since employees with crippling physical disabilities are not effective employees. I feel like there should also be a reset somewhere so you can do different combinations of features rather than just additively.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been struggling with this one a lot, and I really feel that this one is too 'perfect.' It sounds far too much like something that someone would want. While MCD can have some items that are very appealing, I always felt that they had a strong subtext that made them more disturbing. I think that the source of a large part of my problem would be the sales pitch. It just contributes too much to a tone I find problematic. Downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually made a surprised 'eugh!' sound when I saw the picture, which would make this the first SCP to register a noticeable physical response from me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BRILLIANT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So uh, Roget. You forgot to expunge option nine. The form is supposed to include "horrible setting/effects that are expunged." you appear to have elaborated. You have 24 hours to correct this, or you will be arrested for heresy. (I knossos that's an overreaction.)

Oh and I up voted. Both for the reason above, and I like it in general.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, more MC&D. I remember when I tried to write one *shivers*.

I like it, but there's a couple things:

The coffin

"SCP-1725 is an ornate brass coffin…" You don't introduce it as a coffin, you introduce it as a box. Also, introduce the command terminal as foreshadowing for Setting Nine.

Hair will grow long, with the 'hair' is grown from tendrils of the subjects skin, and cause extreme pain when touched.

I can guess what is happening, but still bad sentence structure.

and will gain massive restructuring of the face and body to make it more 'elvin'

It makes you look like a Jazz drummer from the sixties? I'm pretty sure it's "elfin".

All and all, this is highly disturbing. Good Job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is interesting and well executed, but the amount of suffering the servants experience from the mutations seems somewhat gross. It may be beneficial to tone that feature down slightly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in the sandbox, it's getting an upvote here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
" And if the staff ever start getting uppity about their predicament…give them some time with option nine. "

Okay, this SAID ten at the end, someone's messing with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, for setting 9, what does the operator use to have "full control" over the person being punished? All that is mentioned is the dial, and if they used that there would be no purpose for a separate "Punish" setting. Is there another interface?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um, what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well knowing the picture is 100% fake kind of ruins that aspect, plus the actual features of the item just reminds me of all the other body-reshaping devices except it has controls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The box doesn't list this "option ten." Is it supposed to be there, just [REDACTED] and [DATA EXPUNGED]?
